name: Kopf (Kubernetes Operators Framework)

auth.notes: Uses Kubernetes RBAC via kubeconfig/service account; no separate auth

performance.notes: Event-driven; reaction latency ~100ms from K8s event to handler execution

agent_readiness.notes: Python framework for building Kubernetes operators and controllers. Decorator-based handlers for create/update/delete/field-change events on Kubernetes resources. Automatic reconciliation, error handling, progress tracking. Admission webhooks. Custom Resource Definition management. More Pythonic than Go operator-sdk. Used when teams prefer Python over Go for K8s automation.

gotchas: Python performance less than Go operator-sdk for high-throughput operators
Memory overhead higher than Go operators
Complex reconciliation logic requires careful state management
Less community examples than Go-based operators
best_when: Building Kubernetes operators when team prefers Python over Go
Custom controller logic for database operators, ML training jobs, etc.
avoid_when: High-performance operators managing thousands of resources
Teams with strong Go expertise (operator-sdk more idiomatic)
Simple kubectl automation (shell scripts or Python client-go bindings simpler)
summary: Kopf is a Python framework for building Kubernetes operators and controllers. Decorator-based event handlers for create/update/delete events. Automatic reconciliation, error handling, and admission webhooks. More Pythonic than Go operator-sdk. Best for teams wanting to write K8s operators in Python. Used for database operators, ML training schedulers, and custom resource automation.
